Di`Zok Oracle Shillelagh  
 

Lore Item No Pet Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 30
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +5 MANA: +20
Required level of 51.
Effect: Togor's Insects (Combat, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 3.0 Size: LARGE
Class: SHM
Race: BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

I'm afraid this is no longer pet usable. It's been flag so a pet can no use it any longer. Check out http://forum.magecompendium.com/viewtopic.php?t=18203&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=0

On page 2 Brenlo states its an intended change and more items will be added later at thier discression.

▲ Page top
ranger slow proc weapon
QuoteReply
# Aug 16 2003 at 9:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760388312
1 post
Anyone know a 1HS weapon that a wood elf ranger can use that procs a slow spell? Thanks! )

▲ Page top
RE: ranger slow proc weapon
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2003 at 2:33 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754055236
Sage
140 posts
*cough* Nope... Not-a-one... Ok, ok, /sarcasm off. Besides our epic (Earthcaller) which procs a 50% slow called "Earthcall" there is also the Willsapper from Vaniki in DN, however it only procs 35% slow and is Piercing. If you ask most Rangers, including myself, it's far less frustrating to get the right group together for a Vaniki run than to kill the thousands of denizens of Hate it usually takes to get the Earthcaller, you also get the damage bonus from WS if dual weilded with Swiftwind. EC has better slow, better stats, and is less often resisted, but the slow it procs doesn't last as long as WS. There is also a DoT component to the EC.
I'm not suggesting you bypass the EC for the WS, but while you're making multi-weekly runs to Hate for the Emerald, you might take that same group to Dragon Necropolis once or twice just to hold you over if you really want a 1H slowing weapon.
Best of luck.
▲ Page top
RE: ranger slow proc weapon
QuoteReply
# Nov 24 2003 at 4:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246977530
19 posts
Wilsapper slow is 50 percent, not 35.
▲ Page top
RE: ranger slow proc weapon
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2003 at 1:24 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754055236
Sage
140 posts
Also, note that the Earthcaller has a DOT component in the slow, so watch it with mezzers.

Edited, Sat Oct 25 14:19:01 2003

# Feb 06 2003 at 7:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1676852282727
34 posts
you definately have to be above level 50. I bought one and it gave me the message (I'm in my 40's). I let my friend try it, she's a level 54 barbarian shaman, same race, diety, and class as me and she was able to equip it
▲ Page top
My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Jan 14 2003 at 7:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776410526
Sage
280 posts
Yes this weapon is no good till 51 when u get Turgur's but it may save you in the upper levels. Imagine a horrible pull at low mana. You cast Tigirs Insects ( AE level 58 50% slow ), and equip your SCHW or other haste item and haste yourself or get a chanter to haste you. Voila, you are now hasted between 71-100%, which drops the delay on this weapon to 15-19 or so, and the procs will happen in the first few swings of the fight. At level 58 this slows 68%, which is very good slow rate. As a primary slot item and +5 WIS +20 mana is better than alot of things if you dont have the epic yet, and one handed means you still get to equip a sheild. Yes its very situational, but I still carry mine. Definately worth the 500pp I paid for it. BTW, this is the ONLY item in the game with this proc according to Allakhazams. Its worth 500pp just for the 5 WIS 20 mana even if u cant use the effect and dont melee.

58 Ogre Luminary on Luclin server--Chaotic Winds--Baron Aggrok
▲ Page top
RE: My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2003 at 9:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762612188
111 posts
Forget the haste and beef-up DEX with a buff. Haste has no bearing on a proc. For reference, you can check out most class websites.

If you want a weapon to proc, go DEX.

Amose
▲ Page top
RE: My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2003 at 9:39 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592246534719
View User Forum
Scholar
41 posts
While you are right, Amose, to say that Dex is what increases the chances of a weapon proccing - haste is very important too. A weapon can only proc when you swing it - and the more often and faster you swing it, the more often and sooner it can proc.

So dont forget the dex, but dont forget the haste either.
▲ Page top
RE: My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2004 at 9:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1600647551617
282 posts
Haste won't let a weapon proc more often in a battle. Doesn't matter if you're slowed or hasted, if your dex determines that you'll proc only 3 times per battle, that's all you'll proc. Doesn't matter if you take 30 swings or 10.
▲ Page top
RE: My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2004 at 1:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786396881
Scholar
36 posts
How can your dex know how long a battle will last before it ends?

If you have a 255 Dex you might have a 20% chance of proc with each swing (Example numbers, I dont know the real chance to proc). If you are swinging at the usual 30 delay you will proc within 15 seconds of beginning to swing on average. However, if you are 50% hasted you will swing every 2 seconds and thus can count on a proc within 10 seconds. 100% haste brings this down to 8 seconds on average. So, as you can see haste really does matter, unless I'm not understanding procs correctly.
▲ Page top
RE: My 2 coppers
QuoteReply
# Mar 11 2004 at 2:15 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591783493403
131 posts
Well you do not understand procs correctly

Each weapon has a PPM value (procs per minute) that is a function of a "base innate" value and your DEXTERITY (some PoTime mods and the Weapon Affinity also modify proc rates but it's irrevelant here).

Then, you factor in this PPM the delay of your weapon (post hastes/slow), PPM/delay gives you the % chance of proc per hit

Hence the slower you hit the more you'll proc per hit, the faster you hit (haste) the less you'll proc per hit

This has been tested a lot on the Warrior boards (steelwarrior) and every high end weapon statistics (dps, ...) is now given with the PPM@305 dex, not the Proc Per Hit.
Also note that this fact lead to warrior AE groups:
Warriors have an AE rampage AA, completely useless, but some people found that you could still proc on a rampage hit, they used that + Earthshaker (AE DD proc) against swarms of mobs, when slowed they'd get about 80% proc rate (aka 50 mobs = 40 AEs), around 40-60% proc rate (20 to 30 AEs per 50 mobs pulled).
Difference is noticeable and counts as a proof

Edited, Thu Mar 11 02:17:03 2004
▲ Page top
Proc on Shilellagh
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2002 at 9:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361356663
5 posts
Hail All,

This weapon would be nice if we could use it before reaching lvl 51. As it stands it can only be used from 51 on, thus making this weapon useless to the twinkers. My only problem is with the proc. The spell, Togor's Insects, is a lvl 39 slow spell. In most cases a lvl 51 shaman is too busy participating in raids where mobs average in lvl from 50 to 65, I doubt a lvl 39 slow spell is REALLY going to do much help, especially if there is a good chance it will be resisted. I now have this weapon and at lvl 43 I'll have to keep it in the bank until I can use it. It is a good melee weapon though because even if it does have delay 30, shamans can cast a haste spell on themselves that can reduce the delay greatly. My two coppers...



Edited, Tue Nov 26 21:17:40 2002

i bought one these on Ab for 2k and pair cobalt gauntlets(about 3k worth on AB)

ANd its been waste of money (

This weapon is too slow to fight well with, and trying to rely on it procing to slow the mob means ur gonna get wasted!!
I have a club of the ice ocean ~ FAR superior weapon !!
Only use for this club is if ur low on mana and ur despweerate to slow a mob thats creamin the main tank- fought like this few times in velks: lol once was with a ranger and a rogue pullin spiders- i was healer and slower hehehe as u can probably guess it was hard work!

Not Bad!
QuoteReply
# Oct 11 2001 at 10:43 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756863518
View User Forum
132 posts
It seems that alot of weapons and such for all classes are a bit stale, such as warriors getting swords, shamans spears, etc etc etc. Good to see a new item for shamans that can compare to the spears. Hands down this is worth dropping 1 Wis for my Spine chill for an incredible proc! Itrs just not cool its a random proc, this would be awesome if it was triggered. To give you a idea how much shammys would love it, at 42 Togor's costs me about 1m with high spec alt, so thats a whole bubble of mana free for something else!

Reminds me of...
QuoteReply
# Oct 11 2001 at 7:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245593148
16 posts
Truncheon of Doom, a 2handed blunt weapon for warrior's only that drops in Sebilis. 33 damage 44 delay and procs Tagar's Insects at level 50. So shammies now get a 1 hand equivalent with a wis and mana bonus. 1 hand wis items that you can still combat with are always nice for shaman and it gives them an excuse to actually use 1h blunt instead of piercing all the time.

Stick not wanna work fer da barbarians mebee. We tried to gib to a lebel 40 male barb and a lebel 48 female barb and neidder wun of dem kud use it. We tink dat da stick gots bugz. Will petishin dis as soon as me kin.

My guild pulled one of these out of Chardok Friday night. Since there wasnt a Shaman in the party they turned it over to me until we can decide what to do with it. It didnt say anything about level, but is this the message that pops up for wrong level also? I may be posting to answer my own question, as I intend on petitioning this when I log in today, and id like to hear what you were told also Honeypot.
